calcom/cal.com#9485.body:
### Issue Summary

created from https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/issues/3457#issuecomment-1581465501, but I am also using Fastmail and seeing the same issues. This is making it so I can't use the integration.

We also use Fastmail and have been seeing some oddities with the [cal.com](http://cal.com/) integration. Namely, that participants are receiving multiple calendar invites, which on it's own isn't that big of a deal. However, the Fastmail invites are always in UTC, not in the local time of the cal.com invite.

This is creating a lot of confusion with participants, they keep missing our meetings, not realizing one invite is UTC and one in local time. I spoke with the Fastmail team and they relayed the following notes about cal's specific integration.

1. There is a property (SCHEDULE-AGENT=CLIENT) a CalDAV client can add to the event that would prevent CalDAV servers from sending invitations. It appears cal.com is not setting this property even though they are sending an invitation.
2. The invitation being sent by [cal.com](http://cal.com/) is using a different UID. Basically every event has a UID that uniquely identifies the event. Since [cal.com](http://cal.com/) is sending the invitation of the event with a different UID, if you end up adding this event to your calendar [cal.com](http://cal.com/), it will create a duplicate event in your calendar as well. They need to fix it so that all emails associated with a single event use the same original UID.
3. The event added from [cal.com](http://cal.com/) doesn't have a timezone associated with it. Though it mentions the invitees timezone as "America/Chicago" in the notes, the event itself doesn't include a timezone and only specifies the time in UTC. Since the event time is specified in UTC, Fastmail's invitation email will also be sent in UTC.
4. There seems to be no way to stop Cal.com from creating the calendar events at all, and thus no real workaround.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Connect to Fastmail using CalDav integration
2. Create a test booking
3. See issues above.

Any other relevant information. For example, why do you consider this a bug and what did you expect to happen instead?
1. SCHEDULE-AGENT=CLIENT should be set, so Fastmail knows not to send an invitation. **This is the main one.**
2. UID should be unique per event.
3. Event time should have a timezone associated with it.
4. An option could be added to make calendar event creation optional (nice-to-have, not essential).

### Technical details

None other than Fastmail/CalDAV.
calcom/cal.com#9485.title: CalDAV integration with Fastmail is generating duplicate, erroneous invitation emails.

calcom/cal.com#16651.body:
I propose integrating a feature in Cal.com that allows users to set default Google Calendar notifications for events booked through the platform. This functionality should enable users to select preferred notification times (e.g., 10 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour before the event) that automatically apply to all events created via Cal.com bookings. Currently, the absence of such a feature leads to missed appointments since default notifications set in Google Calendar do not apply to these events. Integrating this feature would enhance user experience by ensuring consistency in how notifications are handled, regardless of how an event is booked.

calcom/cal.com#16797.body:
### Is your proposal related to a problem?

Yes, the Pipedrive integration in Cal.com is currently not functioning because it relies on Revert.dev, which has been deprecated and no longer maintained since September 2023. When I try to add Pipedrive via the web UI, I receive an error message stating "pipedrive client id missing."

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like the Pipedrive integration to be updated to remove the dependency on Revert.dev. Instead, the integration should directly use Pipedrive's API, implementing OAuth2 authentication. This would involve updating the web UI to allow users to input their Pipedrive Client ID and Client Secret to establish the connection.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I've considered manually integrating Pipedrive using custom API scripts, but this approach is not practical for most users due to its complexity. I also looked into third-party integration tools, but they may not offer the same seamless experience and could incur additional costs.

### Additional context

Maintaining a functional Pipedrive integration is crucial for users who rely on it to sync their scheduling with their CRM systems. The deprecation of Revert.dev has disrupted this workflow, impacting productivity. Updating the integration will restore this essential functionality for many users.

### Requirement/Document

- [Pipedrive API Authentication Documentation](https://pipedrive.readme.io/docs/core-api-concepts-authentication)
calcom/cal.com#16797.title: Native Pipedrive integration

calcom/cal.com#20703.body: ### Is your proposal related to a problem? Yes. The 12h/24h toggle button on the booking page is missing an accessible name or ARIA label. This makes it unusable for screen reader users, as the control is not announced properly and its purpose is unclear. This violates accessibility guidelines and impacts the user experience for people relying on assistive technologies. ### Describe the solution you'd like Add an appropriate aria-label (e.g., aria-label="12-hour or 24-hour time format toggle") to the toggle button so screen readers can accurately convey the control's function to users. Alternatively, consider using a visually hidden label that also improves accessibility without changing the visual layout. Original Code: ``` ``` Improved Code: ``` ``` ### Describe alternatives you've considered * Using custom JavaScript to add an ARIA label client-side. This is a fragile and unsustainable workaround, especially across updates. * Ignoring the issue and accepting reduced accessibility — not a viable option for inclusive design or for deployments that must meet WCAG or legal accessibility requirements. ### Additional context This button is a interactive element during booking and should be accessible. Proper labeling ensures that all users, including those using screen readers, can understand and interact with it. ### Requirement/Document https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/name-role-value.html

calcom/cal.com#22365.body:
Found a bug? Please fill out the sections below. 👍

### Issue Summary

On a fresh, self-hosted installation of Cal.com v5.4.14, core features that rely on email (Password Reset) or cryptography (2FA Setup) are failing, despite the application container starting successfully and reporting a ✓ Ready state. The "Forgot Password" feature fails to send an email, and enabling 2FA results in a "Something went wrong" error.

This occurs on a new 2GB RAM server with a clean configuration that has been meticulously checked. The root cause has been isolated to the Docker container being unable to make outbound network connections on port 587 (for SMTP), even though the host server can connect without issue. This points to a fundamental networking issue between the Docker container and the host system that persists despite applying all standard firewall and Docker networking fixes.


### Steps to Reproduce

1. Provision a new Ubuntu 22.04 server (2GB RAM).

2. Install Docker, Docker Compose, and Caddy.

3. Configure ufw, allowing ports 80, 443, and 22. Set DEFAULT_FORWARD_POLICY="ACCEPT" and add the standard Docker override rules to /etc/ufw/before.rules.

4. Deploy Cal.com v5.4.14 and Postgres using a standard docker-compose.yml file.

5. Configure all required environment variables, including valid SMTP credentials using Zoho Mail and an App-Specific Password.

6. Run docker compose up -d. The containers start successfully.

7. Create the initial admin user.

8. Attempt to use the "Forgot Password?" feature on the login page.


Any other relevant information. For example, why do you consider this a bug and what did you expect to happen instead?

### Actual Results

- The password reset email is never sent.

- The Cal.com application log shows a SEND_PASSWORD_RESET_EMAIL_ERROR Error: Connection timeout with code ETIMEDOUT.

- A telnet smtp.zoho.com 587 command executed from inside the calcom_web container hangs at Trying... and eventually times out.


### Expected Results

- The password reset email should be successfully sent via the configured Zoho SMTP server.

- The Docker container should have outbound network access on port 587, allowing it to connect to the SMTP server.


### Technical details

- Cal.com Version: v5.4.14

- Database: postgres:14

- OS: Ubuntu 22.04 on DigitalOcean (2GB RAM)

- Reverse Proxy: Caddy

- Key Log Error: SEND_PASSWORD_RESET_EMAIL_ERROR Error: Connection timeout { code: 'ETIMEDOUT', command: 'CONN' }


### Evidence

Testing Method: The issue was reproduced consistently on a fresh server build where every component (firewall, Docker, Caddy, Cal.com config) was set up step-by-step. The final diagnosis was confirmed by using docker exec to get a shell inside the running calcom_web container and running telnet smtp.zoho.com 587, which proved the container itself was blocked from making the connection, while the host server was not. This isolates the problem to the container's networking layer.
calcom/cal.com#22365.title: Persistent 2FA/SMTP Failure on Self-Hosted Docker Install (v5.4.14)

calcom/cal.com#22822.body: ### Issue Summary **RangeError: Invalid key length in crypto.ts - Incorrect encoding used for CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY** The symmetric encryption/decryption functions in `packages/lib/crypto.ts` are using incorrect encoding (`latin1`) to decode the base64-encoded `CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY` environment variable. This causes a "RangeError: Invalid key length" error when trying to create AES256 ciphers, as the key length becomes 44 bytes instead of the required 32 bytes for AES256 encryption. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Set up a Cal.com development environment with a properly configured `.env` file 2. Ensure `CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY` is set to a base64-encoded 32-byte key (e.g., `SW24Ctnk0foJJkBDsdIr4mW2n/kHOEFRfhU7bSbZ4Jc=`) 3. Start the development server with `yarn dev` 4. Try to add an ICS Feed Calendar integration by making a POST request to `/api/integrations/ics-feedcalendar/add` 5. The error occurs during the encryption process in the API handler ### Actual Results - Application throws `RangeError: Invalid key length` at line 18 in `crypto.ts` - Error occurs in `crypto.createCipheriv(ALGORITHM, _key, iv)` call - Stack trace shows the error originates from `symmetricEncrypt()` function - ICS Feed Calendar integration fails to save credentials - Console shows: `code: 'ERR_CRYPTO_INVALID_KEYLEN'` ### Expected Results - Encryption/decryption functions should work correctly with base64-encoded keys - ICS Feed Calendar integration should successfully save encrypted credentials - No crypto-related errors when using properly configured environment variables - The 32-byte base64 key should be correctly decoded to exactly 32 bytes for AES256 ### Evidence
calcom/cal.com#22822.title: Encryption fails with "Invalid key length" due to incorrect key encoding in crypto.ts

Found a bug? Please fill out the sections below. 👍

### Issue Summary

There is an inconsistency in phone number formatting across different country codes in the phone input component. The bug is caused by the way phone numbers are parsed and formatted using the `libphonenumber-js` library without enforcing a standardized formatting pattern. This leads to unpredictable spacing and grouping of numbers depending on the country code.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to the phone number input field in the app (`packages/features/components/phone-input/PhoneInput.tsx`).
2. Enter a phone number with a country code, e.g., `+58XXXXXXXXXX`.
3. Observe the format of the number displayed.
4. Change the country code to `+1` or `+44` and enter the respective number.
5. Compare the displayed format across different country codes.

### Actual Results

- Phone numbers appear in inconsistent formats depending on the country code.
- Some country codes add extra spacing or different grouping of digits.
- The "+" prefix is always added, sometimes resulting in unexpected formatting.

### Expected Results

- Phone numbers should follow a consistent, standardized format regardless of the country code.
- Ideally, the formatting should align with the E.164 standard or a uniform international format for all numbers.

### Technical details

- Files involved:
  - `packages/lib/formatPhoneNumber.ts`
  - `packages/features/components/phone-input/PhoneInput.tsx`
- Code snippet causing the issue (from `formatPhoneNumber.ts`):

```ts
export const formatPhoneNumber = (phoneNumber: string) => {
  const parsedPhoneNumber = parsePhoneNumberWithError(phoneNumber);
  return parsedPhoneNumber?.isValid() ? parsedPhoneNumber.formatInternational() : phoneNumber;
};
```

- The issue occurs because:
  - `PhoneInput.tsx` automatically adds a "+" prefix to all numbers.
  - `formatPhoneNumber` relies on `libphonenumber-js` which applies different spacing/grouping rules per country code.
  - No standardized formatting pattern is enforced.

- Environment details:
  - Browser: [Add your browser version here]
  - Node.js version: [Add your Node.js version here]
  - No backend involvement — purely frontend formatting issue

### Evidence

- Testing performed manually by entering numbers for multiple country codes (+58, +1, +44).
- Screenshots/videos should be attached to show the inconsistency (e.g., different spacing applied).

This bug is self-contained and limited to the phone input and formatting logic. Fixing it would significantly improve the consistency and user experience of the phone number input feature.

### Summary

Adding attendees to existing bookings via the API returns a **403 Forbidden** error, preventing users from adding new participants. Additionally, **v2 API** does not currently support adding attendees at all, forcing reliance on **v1**, which also fails to send invitation or confirmation emails to the newly added attendee.

* The API should respond with a 200 OK or 201 Created.

### **Actual Result**

* API returns a 403 Forbidden error.
* No attendee is added to the booking.
* No confirmation or invitation emails are sent.

### **Impact**

* **Severity:** S1 (Critical) — This issue blocks a customer demo and delays potential revenue.
* **Users affected:** All API users attempting to manage attendees for existing bookings (particularly enterprise and integrations).

### 

**Notes**

* v1 allows attendee creation but lacks email notifications.
* v2 currently does **not** support adding attendees to existing bookings.
* Related request: prioritizing attendee management via **v2 API**.
* Issue is time-sensitive — customer waiting for resolution to proceed with integration and payment.
* Confirmed reproducible using valid API keys and booking IDs.

calcom/cal.com#24325.body:
Sure, this is not a software defect as such, but being (IMO) it incorrect, I'm filing this as a bug.

### Issue Summary

`README.md` states that:

> If you plan to distribute the code, keep the source code public to comply with [AGPLv3](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/LICENSE).

Complying with the AGPLv3 **does not** require the distributed code to be public. It just requires the source code (modified or not) to be given to any user of that downloaded software that requests it. While this is technically (and more easily) accomplished by making it public, that's not the obligation.

> To clone in a private repository, [acquire a commercial license](https://cal.com/sales)

This is against the very definition of OSS. Any OSS software can, by definition, be cloned and used privately, without any limitations. 

### Evidence

* The AGPLv3 license text (linked from README itself).
* [The essential freedoms of Free Software](https://www.gnu.org/philosophy/free-sw.en.html)
calcom/cal.com#24325.title: Incorrect statements regarding the use of AGPLv3 license

calcom/cal.com#24780.body:
## What does this PR do?

Implements auto-accept logic for team membership creation in **API v2 only** based on matching email domains with organization settings. When a user's email domain matches the organization's `orgAutoAcceptEmail` setting (and the org is verified), their team membership is automatically accepted and they are immediately added as hosts to team event types with `assignAllTeamMembers=true`.

**Key Behavior**: This PR ALWAYS overrides `accepted` to `true` when the email domain matches, even if the API caller explicitly passes `accepted: false`. This ensures consistent auto-acceptance and prevents pending memberships for matching users.

**Scope**: Changes apply to API v2 invitation flow only. TRPC invitation flow remains unchanged (case-sensitive exact match without trimming) as no issues have been reported with current behavior.

Fixes #24780

---

## Architecture

**New Service Layer:**
- `OrganizationMembershipService`: Core auto-accept logic with email domain matching
- `PrismaOrganizationRepository`: Fetches organization auto-accept settings
- Dependency injection setup with proper module tokens

**Integration:**
- API v2 `OrganizationsTeamsMembershipsController` calls service before creating membership
- Auto-accepted users trigger immediate event type host assignment via `updateNewTeamMemberEventTypes`

**Domain Matching Rules:**
- Case-insensitive comparison (RFC 5321 compliant)
- Whitespace trimmed from both user email domain and org setting
- Exact match only (no subdomain support: `user@dept.acme.com` will NOT match `acme.com`)
- Requires `isOrganizationVerified: true`

---

## Testing

**E2E Tests Added** (`organizations-teams-memberships.controller.e2e-spec.ts`):
1. ✅ Auto-accept when email matches orgAutoAcceptEmail
2. ✅ Case-insensitive matching (`bob@ACME.COM` matches `acme.com`)
3. ✅ Override behavior (always accept when matched, even if `accepted: false` passed)
4. ✅ No auto-accept for non-matching domains
5. ✅ Event type host assignment for auto-accepted users

**Test Data Required:**
- Organization with `orgAutoAcceptEmail` set and `isOrganizationVerified: true`
- Users with matching and non-matching email domains
- Team with event type having `assignAllTeamMembers: true`

**Expected Behavior:**
- Matching users: `accepted: true`, immediately added as event type hosts
- Non-matching users: `accepted: false`, not added to event types

---

## Important Notes for Reviewers

### ⚠️ Critical Behavior to Verify

**Override Decision**: When email matches, the service ALWAYS sets `accepted: true`, even if caller passes `accepted: false`. This is intentional to ensure auto-accepted users immediately receive bookings for team events. Confirm this is the desired product behavior.

**API v2 vs TRPC Divergence**: 
- API v2 (this PR): Trimming + case-insensitive comparison
- TRPC flow: Exact match, case-sensitive (unchanged)

This divergence is intentional. No issues reported with TRPC's current behavior, so it remains unchanged to avoid unnecessary complexity.

### Domain Matching Limitations

- **No subdomain support**: `user@sales.acme.com` will NOT match `acme.com`
- **Whitespace handling**: Runtime trimming only, no data migration for existing settings
- **Case sensitivity**: RFC 5321 compliant case-insensitive comparison

### Future Work

TODO comment added at line 173: Refactor to use `inviteMembersWithNoInviterPermissionCheck` when moved to a Service to reduce logic duplication between API v2 and TRPC paths (if TRPC is updated in the future).

---

## Mandatory Tasks

- [x] I have self-reviewed the code
- [x] I have updated the developer docs in /docs if this PR makes changes that would require a documentation change. **N/A** - Internal service implementation, no public API changes requiring docs
- [x] I confirm automated tests are in place that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works.

---

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [x] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [x] I have checked that my changes generate no new warnings
- [x] No Prisma usage outside repositories (follows repository pattern)

---

calcom/cal.com#24936.body:
## Problem

When making a request to GET `/event-types/:eventTypeId`in `apps/api/v2/src/ee/event-types/event-types_2024_06_14/controllers/event-types.controller.ts`  if:

1. We pass system wide admin auth then we can't access it because `/event-types/:eventTypeId` checks userId of auth user and then using that and event type id finds the event and since system admin does not own it nothing is returned.
2. If the ":id" in `/event-types/:eventTypeId` is a team event id then again we don't return anything because for that users are expected to use either endpoint:
   1. [https://cal.com/docs/api-reference/v2/orgs-teams-event-types/get-team-event-types](https://cal.com/docs/api-reference/v2/orgs-teams-event-types/get-team-event-types)
   2. [https://cal.com/docs/api-reference/v2/teams-event-types/get-an-event-type](https://cal.com/docs/api-reference/v2/teams-event-types/get-an-event-type)

So in order to support use cases where user needs to resolve team event type based on id and auth (aka is user org or team admin) to provide more flexibility to customers. Our pattern encourages using one of the 2 endpoints above but we should not limit our customers like in this case it's what they want to accomplish but it's not possible right now, because their product asks their users cal api key and event type id so we need to make this happen.

## Solution

1. Moved [bookings.service.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/24969/files#diff-9b7ebe4caa11f4c9d897bdb952f673d3d8d4c20090bf0036dfdb75764a0f637f) function `userIsEventTypeAdminOrOwner` to separate service [event-type-access.service.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/24969/files#diff-987b3f1157245128e42693ce1bb01f8afef5ba796a779ea39a97e7baee11513a)

calcom/cal.com#25088.body: ### Issue Summary When using the `Booker` atom component and passing in multiple values to the `username` prop to do a group meeting, the `/v2/slots/available` request never gets called. ### Steps to Reproduce This happens when rendering the `Booker` atom component with multiple `username`s. Example component: ```tsx { console.log('booking created successfully', data); }} /> ``` ### Actual Results The `Booker` component doesn't completely load if the fetched event type is a dynamic event type (ID: 0) and thus, fetching the available slots doesn't happen. ### Expected Results The `Booker` component should fetch the available slots if the event type was fetched, in this case, it is the dynamic event type (ID: 0). ### Technical details - Tested on Chrome - Node.js version 12.14 - React version 18.3.1 - This is on `@calcom/atoms` version 1.12.1 ### Evidence This is what the Booker component looks like when it doesn't fetch the available slots: When only 1 username is provided in the `username` array, then the `/v2/slots/available` request shows up normally: --- Please note that I was able to get this to work locally by updating a condition from the bundled `index-[hash].js` in `node_modules/@calcom/atoms/dist`. Checking the code, it would be the file `packages/platform/atoms/booker/BookerPlatformWrapper.tsx`: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/9d397428028fe5c74375fec98f9d3e75f60e4b48/packages/platform/atoms/booker/BookerPlatformWrapper.tsx#L298-L303 The condition `Boolean(event?.data?.id)` doesn't work well with the dynamic event type since it has an ID of 0. This prevents the `useAvailableSlots` hook from running properly since it becomes disabled. I haven't checked if this would be good enough, but I suggest that the `enabled` condition for the `useAvailableSlots` hook to be the following: ```js enabled: Boolean(teamId || username) && Boolean(month) && Boolean(timezone) && !event?.isPending && Boolean(event?.data) // <-- instead of Boolean(event?.data?.id) ``` An alternative could be `event?.data?.id != null` to ensure that `event.data.id` is both not `null` and `undefined` and any other value would be allowed including 0 (note that using `!==` would only check `null` in this case). Updating the condition in the bundled file (`node_modules/@calcom/atoms/dist/index-[hash].js`) results in the Booker component properly loading the available slots: Please let me know if further information is necessary, thank you.
calcom/cal.com#25088.title: Booker Atom doesn't load available slots for dynamic event type (group meeting)

## Problem

When rescheduling booking `rescheduledByEmail` is null.

## Solution

1. Storing `rescheduledBy` aka who made rescheduling - made a fix in [input.service.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25103/files#diff-0b6b19aec82cfa9d04de462fb7fb6c4397aa56bfa86b13150ea590dbd8cc6f41) to store `rescheduledBy` in the body.

   1. getRescheduledToUid - for old booking will return new bookings uid. for the new booking will be undefined because it is the new booking.
   2. getRescheduledByEmail - for old booking will simply access `databaseBooking.rescheduledBy` and for new booking will fetch old booking by old bookings uid (new booking has `fromReschedule` pointing to old booking) and then access the rescheduled by email.
3. Add tests in [user-bookings.e2e-spec.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25103/files#diff-ee7a38c8994c1f92984b5a0d73832154989a76baed2dd752e4cb0d7a008229c6) that test:
   1. Rescheduling a booking and checking that the new booking has correct rescheduled by email and reason and rescheduled from uid.
   2. Fetch the new booking and assess the same properties.
   3. Fetch old booking and assess that rescheduled by email is returned and rescheduled to uid (new booking uid)

Remember - rescheduled by is stored in old booking but rescheduling reason in new one.

calcom/cal.com#25125.body:
## What does this PR do?

This PR adds a modular, versioned audit logging infrastructure for booking events. It creates action-specific services for tracking booking lifecycle changes (created, cancelled, rescheduled, etc.) with typed schemas and i18n-ready display methods. The PR integrates audit logging into booking creation, rescheduling, and other booking lifecycle flows.

**Key additions:**
- 12 action-specific audit services with independent versioning (all currently at v1)
- Actor type system to track who performed actions (User/Guest/System)
- Integration with BookingEventHandlerService for multiple booking events
- Helper class for common audit service operations via composition
- Shared change schemas for tracking old→new values

---

## Architecture Overview

Each booking action (CREATED, CANCELLED, RESCHEDULED, etc.) has its own service class that:
1. Defines a versioned schema for the audit data
2. Parses and validates input data
3. Provides i18n-ready display methods for UI rendering
4. Can evolve independently (v1 → v2 → v3) without affecting other actions

The `BookingAuditService` orchestrates all action services and provides a unified interface for creating and reading audit logs.

---

## Current Integration Status

**Wired up:**
- `onBookingCreated` - Logs when bookings are created
- `onBookingRescheduled` - Logs when bookings are rescheduled
- `onRescheduleRequested` - Logs when reschedule is requested
- `onAttendeeAdded` - Logs when guests are added to a booking
- `onLocationChanged` - Logs when booking location is edited
- `onHostNoShowUpdated` - Logs when host is marked as no-show
- `onAttendeeNoShowUpdated` - Logs when attendee is marked as no-show

**Methods defined but not yet called:**
- `onBookingCancelled`
- `onBookingRejected`
- `onBookingAccepted`
- `onAttendeeRemoved`
- `onReassignment`

These will be wired up in follow-up PRs as we integrate audit logging throughout the booking lifecycle.

---

## Known Limitations

1. **Actor system incomplete**: Currently uses a temporary `SYSTEM_ACTOR_ID` fallback in some places because user UUID wiring is not complete. See TODO comments in handlers.

2. **Translation keys not added**: Display methods reference i18n keys like `audit.cancelled_booking`, `audit.added_guests`, etc. These need to be added to translation files in a follow-up.

3. **Seated bookings edge case**: Per `todo.md`, there's a known gap where adding attendees to existing seated bookings doesn't create audit logs (early return at line 1605 in RegularBookingService).

---

## Mandatory Tasks (DO NOT REMOVE)

- [x] I have self-reviewed the code (A decent size PR without self-review might be rejected).
- [x] I have updated the developer docs in /docs if this PR makes changes that would require a documentation change. N/A - internal infrastructure, no user-facing changes.
- [ ] I confirm automated tests are in place that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works. Tests will be added in follow-up PR once integration is complete.

---

## How should this be tested?

**Manual testing:**
1. Create a new booking and verify audit log is created in the database
2. Reschedule a booking and verify audit log captures old/new times
3. Add guests to a booking and verify attendee addition is logged
4. Edit booking location and verify location change is logged
5. Mark host/attendee as no-show and verify it's logged
6. Check that audit logs include correct actor information

**Database verification:**
```sql
SELECT * FROM BookingAudit WHERE bookingUid = '<your-booking-uid>' ORDER BY timestamp DESC;
```

**Environment variables:**
- Standard Cal.com development setup required
- No additional environment variables needed

---

## Checklist

- [x] I have read the [contributing guide](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [x] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [x] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [x] I have checked if my changes generate no new warnings

---

## Human Review Checklist

- [ ] Verify actor system: Some handlers use `makeSystemActor()` due to incomplete user UUID wiring - confirm this is acceptable
- [ ] Error handling: Audit failures are logged but don't throw - verify this doesn't mask issues
- [ ] Type safety: Verify the type guards correctly discriminate audit data types
- [ ] Old/new value capture: Verify each integration correctly captures the previous state before updates

---

**Link to Devin run:** https://app.devin.ai/sessions/afae788e2aa348dc899571d291e116eb  
**Requested by:** @hariombalhara (hariom@cal.com)

calcom/cal.com#25410.body:
## Problem

1. We first returned cancellation reason and cancelled by as `cancellationReason: databaseBooking.cancellationReason || undefined` so if cancellation reason was null we returned it as undefined and then booking output `packages/platform/types/bookings/2024-08-13/outputs/booking.output.ts` would remove `cancellationReason` from response since it is undefined so the property was not returned.
2. This PR [https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/22082/files](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/22082/files) introduced that cancellation reason is returned only for cancelled bookings.
3. But now since rescheduled bookings store cancellation reason also on that field it was no longer returned.

## Solution

Return `cancellationReason: databaseBooking.cancellationReason || ""` so that it is returned as an empty string if `cancellationReason` is null in database and the key exists in response.
calcom/cal.com#25410.title: fix: return cancelled reason even when rescheduled

calcom/cal.com#25494.body:
### **Description**

When the attendee email and host email coincide or are too similar, the system automatically appends a suffix to the original email, resulting in modified addresses such as:

```
aa@aa.com → aa+cmhyhz55c00001tqjyxm9fj3v@aa.com  
attendee1@mail.com → attendee1+{generated_string}@mail.com
```

Customers expect the **original email** to be returned in the API response, not a modified version.

Example response showing unexpected behavior:

"userPrimaryEmail": "Attendee1+cmhyhz55c00001tqjyxm9fj3v@duda.com"

"responses.email": "aa+cmhyhz55c00001tqjyxm9fj3v@aa.com"

### **Expected Behavior**

* API should return the **original email provided by the user**, without adding a suffix.
* Suffixing logic (used internally to prevent collisions) should not overwrite or replace the customer-facing email fields.

### **Acceptance Criteria**

* Email fields in API responses match the exact email provided in the booking payload.
* Suffixing logic only affects internal identifiers and never user-visible fields.
* Booking creation, reminders, notifications, and app integrations continue functioning without regressions.
calcom/cal.com#25494.title: [Bug] Emails are being modified with +{suffix} when attendee & host emails overlap

calcom/cal.com#25503.body:
### **Description**

A customer reported localization issues when using Icelandic, specifically incorrect month and date rendering. Current workaround loads translations from:

https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/tree/main/apps/web/public/static/locales

(based on prior discussion: https://duda.slack.com/archives/C08SQ8YUMM1/p1756718265208519)

We now need to extend language support in Atoms (especially Booker) to include:

* **is – Icelandic** (special case: unsupported by Intl.DateTimeFormat)
* **lt – Lithuanian**
* **nb – Norwegian Bokmål**

Icelandic is failing because Intl.DateTimeFormat.supportedLocalesOf(\['is'\]) → \[\].

FormatJS polyfill partially fixes this but introduces regressions.

Root cause: **UI uses multiple date/time formatting methods**, leading to inconsistent localization.

Examples:

* weekday.ts
* AvailableTimes.tsx

**Request**

Unify date/time localization across Atoms and ensure proper support for new locales, with special handling for Icelandic.
calcom/cal.com#25503.title: [Bug] Localization: Add support for Icelandic, Lithuanian, and Norwegian Bokmål + unify date handling

calcom/cal.com#25700.body: ### Issue Summary The "Hidden" badge indicating that an event type is hidden from the profile is not visible in mobile view. For example, why do you consider this a bug and what did you expect to happen instead? ### Actual Results ### Expected Results ### Technical details - **Browser version**: All browsers (responsive design issue) - **Screen sizes affected**: Viewports < 640px (mobile breakpoint) - **Component**: `apps/web/modules/event-types/views/event-types-listing-view.tsx` - **Root cause**: The badge is rendered inside a container with `hidden sm:flex` classes (line 524), which hides the entire actions area on mobile - **Solution**: Added mobile-specific badge rendering in the `Item` component that displays only on mobile using `sm:hidden` class, positioned next to the event type title

calcom/cal.com#25743.body: ### Issue Summary The PhoneInput component has styling inconsistencies that break focus states and hover behavior across themes. Three issues: 1. Focus border inconsistency: The component uses `dark:focus:border-emphasis`, which only applies in dark mode. In light mode, the focus border doesn't show, making focus state unclear. 2. Focus ring conflict: The component uses `focus-within:ring-2`, which conflicts with the design system's `focus:ring-0` (as in `TextField`). This creates inconsistent focus styling across form inputs. 3. Unwanted hover effects: The flag button and search input have `hover:!bg-emphasis`, causing background color changes on hover that don't match other form inputs and create visual noise. These issues make the PhoneInput look and behave differently from other form inputs, breaking design system consistency and causing a poor user experience, especially in light mode where focus states are unclear. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Navigate to the cal.ai workflow configuration page (Settings → Workflows → Create/Edit workflow). 2. Add a new workflow step or edit an existing one. 3. Select "Send SMS to specific number" or "Send WhatsApp message to specific number" as the action. 4. notice phone input field which has uneven bold ring. ### Actual Results ### Expected Results ### Technical details - Browser version: All modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) - Node.js version: Any version running the application - Theme: Affects both light and dark modes - Component location: `packages/features/components/phone-input/PhoneInput.tsx` - Related components: `TextField` component in `packages/ui/components/form/inputs/TextField.tsx` uses `focus:ring-0` as the standard - CSS classes involved: - `dark:focus:border-emphasis` (should be `focus-within:border-emphasis`) - `focus-within:ring-2` (should be `focus-within:ring-0`) - `hover:!bg-emphasis` on `buttonClass` and `searchClass` (should be removed)
calcom/cal.com#25743.title: fix(phone-input): resolve styling inconsistencies in focus states and hover effects

## Issue Summary

The Add Variables Dropdown component in the text editor had several styling inconsistencies that affected visual design system compliance and user experience:

1. **Focus ring color mismatch**: The search input field used `focus:ring-brand-800` which was too prominent and inconsistent with the design system's subtle focus indicators
2. **Missing border radius**: The dropdown container and menu items lacked rounded corners, creating a visually inconsistent appearance
3. **Redundant hover/active state styling**: The button elements had conditional background color logic that was unnecessary and potentially causing visual glitches

These issues made the component feel inconsistent with the rest of the Cal.com UI and could confuse users during keyboard navigation.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Navigate to any page with a text editor that supports variables (e.g., Workflows, Email Templates, or Custom Email settings)
2. Click on the "Add variable" dropdown button
3. Observe the search input field when it receives focus (either by clicking or tabbing to it)
4. Navigate through the variable list using keyboard arrows or mouse hover
5. Observe the visual appearance of the dropdown container and menu items

## Actual Results

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/18654aeb-94fa-4d32-9330-0a6e2174eccb


## Expected Results

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/23240ca8-345b-4a8c-b543-c20981d46f69

## Technical Details

- **Component**: `packages/ui/components/editor/plugins/AddVariablesDropdown.tsx`
- **Changes made**:
  - Line 145: Changed `focus:ring-brand-800` → `focus:ring-subtle` on Input component
  - Line 148: Added `rounded-md` class to dropdown container div
  - Line 153: Added `rounded-md` class to DropdownMenuItem
  - Line 158: Removed conditional `hover:bg-muted` and `bg-muted` styling from button className
- **Design system**: Follows Cal.com's design tokens for focus states and border radius

calcom/cal.com#26125.body:
## What does this PR do?

Fixes a bug where host records were not being created for event types with `assignAllTeamMembers=true` when a user accepts an organization invite that also auto-accepts pending child team memberships.

**The problem:**
When a user accepts an org invite via `joinAnyChildTeamOnOrgInvite`, the function:
1. Accepts the org membership
2. Accepts ALL pending child team memberships (via `updateMany`)
3. But only called `updateNewTeamMemberEventTypes(userId, org.id)` for the org

Since organizations don't have event types with `assignAllTeamMembers`, this call was effectively a no-op, and the child teams' event types never got their host records created.

**The fix:**
1. Query pending child team memberships before accepting them (using `OrganizationRepository.findPendingChildTeamMemberships`)
2. After the transaction, call `updateNewTeamMemberEventTypes` for each child team that was just accepted

## Updates since last revision

- Refactored to use `OrganizationRepository` instead of inline prisma queries per repository pattern conventions
- Added `findPendingChildTeamMemberships` method to `OrganizationRepository`
- Resolved merge conflict with main (new `findPlatformOrgByUserId` method added on main coexists with `findPendingChildTeamMemberships`)
- Fixed duplicate `findPendingChildTeamMemberships` method introduced during conflict resolution
- Added 2 new edge case integration tests (13 total):
  - **MANAGED scheduling type**: Verifies that child event types (not host records) are created for MANAGED event types with `assignAllTeamMembers=true`
  - **Zero pending child team memberships**: Verifies no host records are created when a user has no pending child team memberships (clean no-op)

## Visual Demo (For contributors especially)

N/A - This is a backend-only data consistency fix with no UI changes.

## Mandatory Tasks (DO NOT REMOVE)

- [x] I have self-reviewed the code (A decent size PR without self-review might be rejected).
- [x] I have updated the developer docs in /docs if this PR makes changes that would require a [documentation change](https://cal.com/docs). N/A - no documentation changes needed.
- [x] I confirm automated tests are in place that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works.

## How should this be tested?

1. Create an organization with a child team
2. Create an event type on the child team with `assignAllTeamMembers=true`
3. Invite a user to the child team (creating a pending membership)
4. Have the user sign up using an org invite token
5. Verify that a `Host` record is created linking the new user to the child team's event type

**Expected behavior:** After accepting the org invite, the user should be added as a host to all child team event types that have `assignAllTeamMembers=true`.

**Integration tests:** Run `TZ=UTC VITEST_MODE=integration yarn test packages/features/auth/signup/utils/joinAnyChildTeamOnOrgInvite.integration-test.ts` — all 13 tests should pass.

## Human Review Checklist

- [ ] Verify `findPendingChildTeamMemberships` correctly filters by `parentId: orgId` and `accepted: false`
- [ ] Confirm `updateNewTeamMemberEventTypes` handles being called for teams where the user is already a host (idempotency) — note: `hosts: { create: [...] }` will throw a unique constraint error if the host already exists. This is a pre-existing concern, not introduced by this PR.
- [ ] Consider if querying memberships before the transaction could cause race condition issues (memberships could be accepted by another process between the query and the transaction) — low risk since org invite acceptance is a one-time operation
- [ ] Note: a comment (`// Simply remove this update when we remove the organizationId field from the user table`) was removed from the transaction block — verify this is intentional

---

Link to Devin run: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/b8e4e38f721e461c8aaa56a2a1c87b28
Requested by: @hariombalhara
calcom/cal.com#26125.title: fix: create host records for child teams when accepting org invite

calcom/cal.com#26140.body:
When deploying Cal.com using the Docker image (calcom/cal.com:latest), the container successfully runs database migrations but fails at the final step of the seeding process. The script /calcom/scripts/seed-app-store.ts throws a ReferenceError because it attempts to access seedAppData, which appears to be undefined in the scope of the script execution.

### Expected behavior
The seed-app-store.ts script should complete without errors, ensuring all default apps and credentials (like mock payment apps or Google calendar configurations) are correctly populated in the database before the Next.js server starts.

### Actual behavior
The script fails with the following stack trace:

```js
calcom-web  | ReferenceError: seedAppData is not defined
calcom-web  |     at /calcom/scripts/seed-app-store.ts:258:5
calcom-web  |     at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
```

calcom/cal.com#26256.body: ### Issue Summary Destructive actions in dropdown menus show a double hover effect: both the parent `DropdownMenuItem`'s subtle gray background and the child `DropdownItem`'s error red background appear on hover. This creates a layered hover state that looks inconsistent and breaks the intended destructive styling. The root cause is that `DropdownMenuItem` applies `hover:bg-subtle` to all items, including those containing destructive `DropdownItem` children. When hovering over a destructive action, both the parent container's subtle background and the child's error background are visible, creating visual conflict. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Navigate to any page with a dropdown menu containing a destructive action (e.g., Settings → API Keys, Teams → Team Members, or Webhooks list) 2. Open the dropdown menu (click the three-dot menu icon) 3. Hover over a destructive action item (e.g., "Delete" with trash icon) 4. Observe the hover state ### Actual Results ### Expected Results ### Technical details - Component: `packages/ui/components/dropdown/Dropdown.tsx` - Issue: `DropdownMenuItem` applies `hover:bg-subtle` unconditionally, conflicting with destructive `DropdownItem`'s `hover:bg-error` - Solution: Detect destructive children in `DropdownMenuItem` and conditionally apply transparent hover background for destructive items, while maintaining subtle hover for non-destructive items **Testing:** - Tested in multiple dropdown menus with destructive actions: - API Keys list (Settings → Developer → API Keys) - Team member actions (Teams → Team Members) - Webhook actions (Settings → Developer → Webhooks) - Booking actions - Event type actions - Verified non-destructive items still show subtle hover background - Confirmed rounded corners and border radius are preserved - Tested in both light and dark modes
calcom/cal.com#26256.title: fix(ui): the dropdown menu has double hover causing a weird bug in the destructive actions on hover.

calcom/cal.com#26271.body: ## Issue Summary The API Keys page in the Developer settings was missing the mobile-responsive floating action button (FAB) design that displays a circular plus icon on mobile devices. This created an inconsistent user experience compared to other pages like the Webhooks page, which correctly shows a plus circle icon on mobile while displaying the full button with text on desktop. The button was using a standard button variant that showed the full "Add" text on all screen sizes, rather than adapting to show just the plus icon on mobile devices for better space utilization and consistency with the design system. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Navigate to Settings → Developer → API Keys 2. View the page on a mobile device (or resize browser to mobile width < 768px) 3. Observe the "Add" button in the header 4. Compare with the Webhooks page (Settings → Developer → Webhooks) on the same device/screen size ## Actual Results ## Expected Results adding the webhook view to in for reference: ## Technical Details - **Screen sizes affected**: Mobile devices and browser widths < 768px - **Component**: `NewApiKeyButton` in `apps/web/modules/settings/developer/api-keys-view.tsx` - **Root cause**: Missing `variant="fab"` and `size="sm"` props on the Button component - **Solution**: Added `variant="fab"` and `size="sm"` props to match the webhooks page implementation
calcom/cal.com#26271.title: fix: Add mobile plus icon to API keys create button to match webhooks page

calcom/cal.com#26274.body: ### Issue Summary The sign out button in the user dropdown menu had two styling issues: (1) it lacked destructive styling (red color) despite being a destructive action, and (2) it had incorrect border radius on the top corners when appearing after the separator. This fix adds `color="destructive"` to apply proper destructive styling (red text with red hover states) and `className="rounded-t-none"` to remove the top border radius, creating a proper visual connection with the separator above it. This makes the sign out button consistent with other destructive actions in dropdown menus throughout the codebase. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Log in to Cal.com 2. Click on the user avatar/profile dropdown in the top navigation 3. Observe the sign out button at the bottom of the dropdown menu (after the separator) 4. Notice that: - The sign out button appears in default text color (not red/destructive) - The sign out button has rounded corners on all sides, including the top, creating a visual gap with the separator ### Actual Results ### Expected Results ### Technical details - Component: `apps/web/modules/shell/user-dropdown/UserDropdown.tsx` - Changes made: 1. Added `color="destructive"` prop to `DropdownItem` (line 206) 2. Added `className="rounded-t-none"` to `DropdownItem` (line 208) - Related component: `packages/ui/components/dropdown/Dropdown.tsx` - The `DropdownItem` component supports `color="destructive"` which applies `text-error` and `hover:bg-error hover:text-error` classes - The `DropdownItem` component applies `rounded-lg` to all corners by default, but when used as the last item after a separator, it should override with `rounded-t-none`

### Issue Summary

On mobile devices, the Installed Apps category pages (/apps/installed/[category]) have a UX issue in the horizontally scrollable category navbar.

calcom/cal.com#26459.body:
### Issue Summary

Cal.com's phone number validation rejects valid Ivory Coast (+225) phone numbers by not accepting the last 2 digits. Ivory Coast migrated to a 10-digit numbering plan in 2021, but the validation system appears to still use the old 8-digit format, preventing Ivorian users from entering their phone numbers correctly.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to any Cal.com form that requires phone number input
2. Select Ivory Coast (+225) as country code
3. Enter a valid 10-digit Ivorian phone number (e.g., `07 97 76 48 77`)
4. Attempt to submit the form

### Actual Results

- The system rejects the last 2 digits of the phone number
- The number `+225 07 97 76 48 77` is marked as invalid
- Users cannot complete the form with their valid Ivorian phone number

### Expected Results

- The system should accept valid 10-digit Ivorian phone numbers in the format `+225 XX XX XX XX XX`
- Phone validation should recognize the current numbering plan implemented in 2021
- Ivorian numbers should be validated the same way as other countries that use 10 digits (e.g., Benin +229, which works correctly)

### Technical details

- Browser: [Ajoutez votre navigateur et version ici]
- The issue appears to be related to the phone validation library (likely libphonenumber) not being updated with Ivory Coast's 2021 numbering plan change
- Comparison: Benin (+229) also uses 10 digits after the country code and is accepted correctly

### Evidence

- Tested with the phone number: `+225 07 97 76 48 77` (valid Ivorian mobile number)
- Comparison test with Benin number: `+229 01 XX XX XX XX` (accepted correctly)
- All Ivorian mobile numbers now start with prefixes 01, 05, 07, etc., followed by 8 more digits (total 10 digits)
Thanks !
calcom/cal.com#26459.title: Phone number validation issue for Ivory Coast (+225) - rejects valid 10-digit numbers

calcom/cal.com#26469.body:
# Problem

Developers have to fill online form and we have to manually create OAuth clients for them and send them credentials, which is tedious.

# Solution

Allow developers to submit OAuth clients that then are reviewed by Cal admins and either approved or rejected: [https://www.loom.com/share/2622b89972034f2d8f3e111b2be6d655](https://www.loom.com/share/2622b89972034f2d8f3e111b2be6d655)

# Implementation

1. We now have two OAuth pages:
   1. Developers can submit for review, edit, view and delete their OAuth clients in the `/settings/developer/oauth` route which displays [oauth-clients-view.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-11bf33c7fe3dd4c6264a3439b7eeb44e1c95dfd3e0e5913ab0b97dc29aeaf46d)
   2. Admins can create new automatically approved OAuth client and view pending, rejected and approved OAuth clients in route `/settings/admin/oauth` which displays [oauth-clients-admin-view.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-82710a7fc221f245380018b91e3b7abd10c5b20d829d25ff4583bd92cf7a43fd)
2. [OAuthClientsList.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-e85b78abea9aa87c38c6a136201a787d83992bb343011bbb09b5c2f6d3149184) - lists OAuth clients by using [listUserClients.handler.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-2b63a2c31bef8947cb9a38619c9f392d68e366cec006ddbc2a273d540eb9c5be) to fetch user OAuth clients and [listClients.handler.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-bcc9e9332a4ff1be0771ec819f468539cb3b0bdc096873bf8fafc07ee172d2b2) to list pending, accepted and rejected OAuth clients on admin page.
3. To create an OAuth client:
   1. [NewOAuthClientButton.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-2e4c4b40081182804f5db3789423d88995581de8c17f6381413fe518d3690896) is clicked to open [OAuthClientCreateModal.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-2c1aa0b70e59849d48857e053800e57002758d1a162190412c2bc777e9434262). Once client is submitted for review / created by admin [OAuthClientPreviewDialog.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-a4b251b6cbefbcb7afa242e812f88eea89f4b55a4d143ef8b52c3f285a59eb14) is displayed to show client name, id and secret that is shown only once at this step.
   2. Admin created OAuth clients by default are approved.
   3. Developer submitted OAuth clients are in a "pending" state and await admin review. [team@cal.com](mailto:team@cal.com) gets [AdminOAuthClientNotificationEmail.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-a3932d58a3f51b196177e9f36eef28e1607f115309fef0e436c42b53dbe08542) email sent.
4. Editing OAuth client:
   1. Only developers can edit their OAuth client. They click on an OAuth client in the [OAuthClientsList.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-e85b78abea9aa87c38c6a136201a787d83992bb343011bbb09b5c2f6d3149184) and then [OAuthClientDetailsDialog.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-d0aec46f48dd9edd3692c178ca17651b339855b11fe0006fde707c91cef2b940) is opened. They can edit their OAuth client or delete it in which case [updateClient.handler.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-5c338d944f639547f9e8ccea38944b4421d8a19cd2b8b82c279dba3d2543c0d7) or [deleteClient.handler.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-89abcc686742731d9fe4828e179dc74aee56bee46fa6ccc6358e078e0f69ecb0) is called respectively.
      1. If developer edits logo, name or website url then admin needs to re-approve the OAuth client.
   2. When OAuth client is edited [updateClient.handler.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-5c338d944f639547f9e8ccea38944b4421d8a19cd2b8b82c279dba3d2543c0d7) is called.
   3. Developer can't update "pkce" being on or off after creating an OAuth client because public ones don't have a secret, so I went with developer having to create a new client if they want to switch between private / public.
5. Since  [OAuthClientCreateModal.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-2c1aa0b70e59849d48857e053800e57002758d1a162190412c2bc777e9434262) and [OAuthClientDetailsDialog.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-d0aec46f48dd9edd3692c178ca17651b339855b11fe0006fde707c91cef2b940) are basically the same (create has no inputs but details one receives name, redirect uri etc. as props) they both re-use [OAuthClientFormFields.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-9ce9c32ca4757d08315ce6e1276d3d589cb35d336ad86cdede13b4577fb4ee40)
6. Admin approving a client - [OAuthClientDetailsDialog.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-d0aec46f48dd9edd3692c178ca17651b339855b11fe0006fde707c91cef2b940) is displayed but admins then see approve and reject buttons. Once it is approved or rejected [updateClient.handler.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-5c338d944f639547f9e8ccea38944b4421d8a19cd2b8b82c279dba3d2543c0d7) is called.
   1. When admin reviews an OAuth client it is only read only - admin can't modify or delete.
   2. If admin approves it then developer receives [OAuthClientApprovedNotificationEmail.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-b1ee7e2a8f998d3c794198f92d3bd9943e8223dee09794af79e53cbd732c6e1c)
   3. If rejected then developer receives [OAuthClientRejectedNotificationEmail.tsx](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-91a9552ee15b548807e3fbcbfeb936374eb7a5288181273088e6b632936013e0)
7. Schema changes - schema.prisma `OAuthClient` model has new fields:
   1. status - pending, approved or rejected. By default it is approved so that all existing OAuth clients are approved.
   2. websiteUrl
   3. purpose - when developer submits an OAuth client he / she must explain how it will be used and provide more context for us when reviewing
   4. rejectionReason - if we reject the submitted OAuth client we provide reason
8. Tests:
   1. e2e web app tests
      1. [oauth-client-owner-crud.e2e.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-9228cc5d5824674420641eaa9c0e474fa167296b14373da0a287154f1131ba3e) tests if user can create OAuth client, access its id and secret and then edit it and delete and that its database state is correct.
      2. [oauth-client-admin.e2e.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-af3ba183ffa515e0d5c564e0bb3b4bfaf43d7e53b7ff0a78c9c9e3ca769f095f)  tests if admin can approve and reject OAuth clients and if they are correctly rendered and if their database state is correct.
   2. handler integration tests
      1. [updateClient.handler.test.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-f56656136273f73d6b7ef8dfe8937d624af468621685c25b65b402797cdb7910)
      2. [submitClientForReview.handler.test.ts](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/25556/files#diff-ec9e2dac072c4a7e30e49a143cd2d19437a43dc5d4ad06db99add126493e2259)

calcom/cal.com#26474.body:
## Problem

If an OAuth client (the one in OAuthClient table) is in review its not usable until the admin approves it.

## Implementation

Allow OAuth client access data of the person who created the OAuth client. After OAuth client has been approved it then can gain access to other cal users' data. Allow access to the owner of the OAuth client even if the client has been rejected by admins - owner can still test it if he / she wants to. Notably, access tokens that are enabled on team level will continue requiring ACCEPTED OAuth client to test - i did not add code for this because team level oauth flows exist only for zapier and there is no toggle anymore to select whether authorization is on team or user level and it will always be on the user level right now.

That means that we have to allow for the owner to:

1. authorize his / her account in the `apps/web/modules/auth/oauth2/authorize-view.tsx` even if the OAuth client is cancelled or pending.
   1. add e2e tests for this in `apps/web/playwright/oauth/oauth-authorize-approval-status.e2e.ts` - we need tests that ensure that if non-owner tries to authorize then errors like now are displayed, so ensure that existing tests use non-owner user as the logged in user authorizing and then add a new describe block where all of the tests with various client statues will pass by allowing the owner to connect via his own client.
2. allow exchanging tokens via the `apps/api/v2/src/modules/auth/oauth2/controllers/oauth2.controller.ts` /tokens endpoint and allow refreshing them if if they belong to the user himself.
   1. test this in apps/api/v2/src/modules/auth/oauth2/controllers/oauth2.controller.e2e-spec.ts and test that non-owner can't do that (i guess existing tests already do that)
3. That probably means updating functions in packages/features/oauth/services/OAuthService.ts that call`ensureClientIsApproved` to pass logged in user from ctx (i think) and then skipping ensureClientIsApproved if logged in user is oauth client owner and then see how these functions like`refreshAccessToken` are called upstream.

### Issue Summary

The yarn installation fails on Windows during the `@calcom/app-store-cli` post-install script due to mixed path separators (backslashes and forward slashes) being passed to Biome.

The `formatFileWithBiome` function in `packages/app-store-cli/src/build.ts` passes Windows-style backslash paths to Biome via `spawnSync`, which Biome cannot process correctly. Also, file path construction uses hardcoded `/` instead of `path.join()`, creating mixed path separators.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Clone the cal.com repository on a Windows machine
2. Run `yarn` to install dependencies
3. Installation fails during the post-install hook

### Actual Results

- Yarn installation fails on windows with exit code 1

### Expected Results

- Yarn should complete the installation successfully on all platforms

calcom/cal.com#26903.body: ## Bug Description On the "Add your details" onboarding page, the Bio textarea has two issues: 1. **Layout Issue**: When the textarea is resized (by dragging the bottom-right corner), the footer buttons overlay the bottom portion of the textarea content, making text invisible and uneditable. 2. **Missing Character Limit**: The Bio field has no character limit or counter, allowing users to enter unlimited text which can cause: - Database issues if there's a backend limit - Poor UX (users don't know the limit) - Performance issues with very long text ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to the onboarding flow (first-time user setup) 2. Navigate to "Add your details" page 3. Type or paste a long text in the Bio field (e.g., Lorem Ipsum paragraph) 4. Drag the bottom-right corner of the Bio textarea to resize it larger 5. Observe that the bottom portion of text is hidden under the Continue/Back buttons 6. Notice there's no character limit or counter ## Expected Behavior - The Bio textarea should have a **maximum height** with internal scrolling - The Bio field should have a **character limit** (e.g., 256 characters) - A **character counter** should show remaining characters (e.g., "45/256") - Footer buttons should never overlay textarea content ## Actual Behavior - Textarea can be resized infinitely - No character limit exists - No character counter visible - Footer buttons overlay textarea content when resized - Users don't know how long their bio can be ## Screenshots ### Problem 1 - brave: Buttons overlay textarea with no text limit ### Problem 2 - chrome: no proper gap between Bio textarea and buttons ## Proposed Fix 1. Add `max-h-[200px] resize-y overflow-y-auto` to limit textarea height 2. Add `maxLength={256}` to enforce character limit 3. Add character counter component showing "X/256 characters" 4. Add proper spacing (`mb-8`) between textarea and footer ## Environment - **Browsers tested**: Chrome, Brave (100% zoom) - **Device**: Desktop

calcom/cal.com#26932.body: ## Problem At the moment, only managed users can use atoms, because the CalProvider atom does not handle yet the oauth tokens for regular users. ## Solution Create a new provider that wraps CalProvider and supports OAuth 2.0 access tokens. Update api v2 endpoints to support this. ## Implementation 1. We need to update the `useOAuthClient` hook because it makes calls to `/v2/provider` endpoint and we also need to create a new endpoint that we can call if we are using OAuth 2.0: 1. [useOAuthClient.ts]() now receives a prop `isOAuth2` that by default is false but if it is true then we call the new endpoint `/atoms/auth/oauth2/clients/${clientId}/provider/${clientId}` found in the new controller [atoms-oauth2.controller.ts]() (it is excluded from docs). 2. `useOAuthClient` also will not set the `"x-cal-client-id"` if `isOAuth2 = true` because api would interpret it as request coming from platform oauth client. 2. The new React provider: 1. Update [BaseCalProvider.tsx]() to accept `isOAuth2` prop that it then passes to `useOAuthClient`. 2. Create the new [CalOAuthProvider.tsx]() that wraps [BaseCalProvider.tsx]() and sets `isOAuth2={true}` 3. Calendars controller: 1. This commit [fix: allow OAuth 2.0 token to connect gcal or ms calendar]() fixes the inability to connect a calendar using OAuth 2.0 access token. 2. This commit [fix: allow OAuth 2.0 token to save gcal or ms calendar credentials]() fixes the inability to save connected calendar credentials. We do not store OAuth 2.0 access tokens in the database so we need to extract the owner id from the access token itself so that we know which cal user should have the new calendar credentials. 4. Emails and calendar events: 1. This commit [fix: calendar events not showing and emails not sent]() fixes emails not being sent and calendar events not being created when making a booking to the old bookings controller. Shortly, up until now if a non-platform customer made booking via this endpoint emails and calendar events were not created due to the default behaviour we had. ## Testing The new readme [README-OAUTH2.MD]() explains how to run the examples app with an OAuth 2.0 token. Here is what I did: 1. [scripts/seed.ts]() now creates an OAuth 2.0 client for the `admin@example.com` user. 2. Once environment variables are set as explained in readme: 1. The examples [\_app.tsx]() will create an OAuth 2.0 user using [oauth2-user.ts]() and set examples app access token. 2. Then [refresh.ts]() will refresh the token by making request to the refresh endpoint of `v2/src/modules/auth/oauth2/controllers/oauth2.controller.ts` If we update time zone in the availability atom: Then it is updated in the main web app:

calcom/cal.com#26996.body:
## Problem

OAuth v2 endpoints don't follow OAuth 2.0 specification.

We also still display in docs next api and not new controller.

## Solution

Make api v2 oauth controller respect OAuth 2.0 specification.

Update documentation to use api v2 endpoints and give code examples

## Implementation

* Removed api v2 /authorize endpoint because it is pointless - authorization always happens in the browser and our next app handles that.
* **Unified token endpoint** — Merged `/exchange` and `/refresh` into `POST /token` with `grant_type` dispatch (`authorization_code` or `refresh_token`)
* **Form-url encoded support** — Token endpoint accepts `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` in addition to JSON
* **Flat token response** — Returns `{ access_token, token_type, refresh_token, expires_in }` directly instead of wrapping in `{ status, data }`
* **RFC error format** — Token errors return `{ error, error_description }` with appropriate HTTP status codes instead of nest.js exceptions
* **client_id in body** — Moved from URL path parameter to request body
* **Cache headers** — Added `Cache-Control: no-store` and `Pragma: no-cache` to token responses
* **snake_case** — DTOs use snake_case field names per OAuth2 spec convention
* OAuth2 endpoints now throw a custom `OAuth2HttpException` caught by a dedicated `OAuth2HttpExceptionFilter`, bypassing the global `HttpExceptionFilter` so that token and authorize error responses return the raw `{ error, error_description }` body required by the OAuth 2.0 spec instead of being wrapped in the platform's standard error envelope.
* The `UrlencodedBodyMiddleware` was added to the `/v2/auth/oauth2/token` route so the token endpoint can accept `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` request bodies, which is the standard content type for OAuth 2.0 token requests — most OAuth libraries send token requests in this format by default, so without this middleware those requests would arrive with an empty body.
* **E2e tests** — Full test coverage for all token and authorize flows including error cases
* added the oauth 2 controller endpoints to swagger documentation
calcom/cal.com#26996.title: refactor: OAuth v2 endpoints

## What does this PR do?

This PR is part of a larger effort to break down PR #26571 into smaller, reviewable chunks. It moves hooks and components from `packages/features/data-table` to `apps/web/modules/data-table` to support the vertical slice architecture.

### Updates since last revision

- Fixed lint error in `CalendarEventBuilder.test.ts`: changed `TimeFormat["TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR"]` to `TimeFormat.TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR` to satisfy the `lint/complexity/useLiteralKeys` rule

- [x] I have updated the developer docs in /docs if this PR makes changes that would require a [documentation change](https://cal.com/docs). N/A - no documentation changes needed.
- [x] I confirm automated tests are in place that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works. Existing tests cover these components.

## How should this be tested?

This is a refactoring PR that moves code without changing functionality. Verify that:

calcom/cal.com#27355.body: ### Issue Summary Self-hosted Cal.com instances with custom logos display the Cal.com default logo instead of their custom logo on OG (Open Graph) images for "generic" type pages (login, generic pages, etc.). This is caused by the `generic` type in `OG_ASSETS` hardcoding `"cal-logo-word-black.svg"` instead of using the `LOGO` constant like `meeting` and `app` types do. In `packages/lib/OgImages.tsx`: - `meeting` type uses `LOGO` constant ✅ - `app` type uses `LOGO` constant ✅ - `generic` type hardcodes `"cal-logo-word-black.svg"` ❌ ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Self-host Cal.com (Docker or any method) 2. Replace `/apps/web/public/logo.png` with a custom logo 3. Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBAPP_URL` to your domain 4. Share a login page URL (e.g., `https://your-domain.com/auth/login`) on WhatsApp, Slack, or Mastodon 5. Observe that the OG image preview shows "Cal.com" logo instead of your custom logo 6. Compare with sharing a meeting booking page URL (e.g., `https://your-domain.com/username/30min`) which correctly shows the custom logo ### Actual Results - Generic pages (login, etc.) show "Cal.com" branding in OG image previews on social media platforms - Meeting booking pages correctly show the custom logo - This creates inconsistent branding for self-hosted instances ### Expected Results - All OG image types (meeting, app, generic) should use the custom logo defined in the `LOGO` constant - Self-hosted instances should have consistent branding across all page types when shared on social media ### Technical details - Cal.com version: 6.1.6 / 6.1.7 - Self-hosted on Docker (Debian 12) - Node.js version: 20 - The `LOGO` constant is already imported at the top of `OgImages.tsx` and used correctly for `meeting` and `app` types **Suggested fix** in `packages/lib/OgImages.tsx` line ~70: ```diff generic: { id: "generic-og-image-v1", - logo: "cal-logo-word-black.svg", + logo: LOGO, logoWidth: "350", variant: "light" as const, }, ``` ### Evidence **Screenshot: WhatsApp preview of login page showing Cal.com logo instead of custom logo:** ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bb002c90-cd10-4df0-9af9-2114b182887f) **OG image generated for generic type showing wrong logo:** **Code evidence - OG_ASSETS in packages/lib/OgImages.tsx:** ```typescript const OG_ASSETS = { meeting: { logo: LOGO, // ✅ Uses constant // ... }, app: { logo: LOGO, // ✅ Uses constant // ... }, generic: { logo: "cal-logo-word-black.svg", // ❌ Hardcoded - BUG // ... }, }; ```
calcom/cal.com#27355.title: [Bug] OG Image for "generic" type ignores custom LOGO constant, always shows Cal.com logo

calcom/cal.com#27465.body:
### Issue Summary

Self-hosting with Docker. After installing, everything was looking good. However, I checked this morning to see that randomly, Google Calendar / Google Meet was disabled on my instance. This was after setting it up and having it work correctly.

I checked the database, and it was properly seeded with the credentials. However, enabled was randomly set to false instead of true. I was able to fix this by updating the SQL table and it seems to be working again. Need to see if it disables again, and if so why that happened.

### TEMPORARY FIX

This bug happens when the recently added `shouldEnableApp` function runs, which happens on container start-up and during the CRON App Sync. Here are the steps to get it working again until a PR is submitted:

1. Spin down the container and add the ENV variable `CRON_ENABLE_APP_SYNC=false`. _This is important to prevent the Calendar from being disabled on a scheduled basis._
2. Run a SQL statement to set the apps enabled status to true, e.g. `UPDATE "App"  SET enabled = true  WHERE slug = 'google-calendar';`
3. If you need to spin down your container again in the future, repeat Step 2. 

It has been 4 days since I submitted this issue and this fix has stopped the calendar apps from consistently breaking.

### Technical details

- On image calcom.docker.scarf.sh/calcom/cal.com:v6.1.9-arm 
- Postgres Alpine 16

calcom/cal.com#27874.body:
### Is your proposal related to a problem?

<!--
  Provide a clear and concise description of what the problem is.
  For example, "I'm always frustrated when..."
-->

Yes.

The README currently contains two separate "License" sections:

1. A detailed Open Core explanation that includes an AGPLv3 vs Enterprise Edition comparison table.

2. A second standalone AGPLv3 license section near the bottom of the file.

This duplication creates redundancy and may cause confusion for readers trying to understand the official licensing structure of the project. It is unclear whether both sections serve distinct purposes or if one unintentionally duplicates the other.

I have reviewed existing issues and did not find a similar report related to duplicate license sections.

### Describe the solution you'd like

<!--
  Provide a clear and concise description of what you want to happen.
-->

I would suggest one of the following improvements:

Merge both sections into a single comprehensive "License" section that clearly explains:
1. Open Core model
2. AGPLv3 licensing
3. Enterprise Edition differences

OR

Remove the duplicate AGPLv3 section at the bottom of the README and instead link back to the primary, detailed license explanation section.

This would make the licensing information more streamlined and easier to understand.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

<!--
  Let us know about other solutions you've tried or researched.
-->

An alternative approach could be:

1. Retaining both sections but clearly distinguishing their purposes with different headings (e.g., "Open Core Licensing Overview" and "Repository License Notice").

2. Adding anchor links at the top of the README to clarify structure.

However, consolidation into a single section appears cleaner and less repetitive.

### Additional context

<!--
  Is there anything else you can add about the proposal?
  You might want to link to related issues here, if you haven't already.
-->
This improvement is purely documentation-related and does not affect functionality.

It aims to:
1. Improve readability
2. Reduce redundancy
3. Prevent confusion regarding AGPL vs Enterprise licensing

If helpful, I am willing to submit a pull request implementing the chosen structure after approval.

### Requirement/Document

<!--
  Is there any type of document that could support that feature?
-->

Reference:

Current README.md file in the main branch of the repository.

No additional documentation is required for this proposal.

---

##### House rules

- If this issue has a `🚨 needs approval` label, don't start coding yet. Wait until a core member approves feature request by removing this label, then you can start coding.
  - For clarity: Non-core member issues automatically get the `🚨 needs approval` label.
  - Your feature ideas are invaluable to us! However, they undergo review to ensure alignment with the product's direction.
  - Follow Best Practices lined out in our [Contributor Docs](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

## Context

In PR https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/26982, a test was added but skipped (`test.skip`) because the behavior is not yet implemented:

```
test.skip("`firstAndLastName` variant to `fullName` when passed as JSON string from URL prefill", ...)
```

**File:** `packages/features/bookings/lib/getBookingResponsesSchema.test.ts` (line 336)

## Problem

When a `name` field with `firstAndLastName` variant is passed as a JSON string from URL prefill (e.g., `name={"firstName":"John","lastName":"Doe"}`), the preprocessing does not currently handle parsing it from a JSON string into the expected object format.

- **Expected output:** `name: "John Doe"` (converted to fullName format)

But this currently does not work because the name field preprocessing does not attempt to parse JSON strings coming from URL query parameters.

## Expected Behavior

The `preprocessNameFieldDataWithVariant` or related preprocessing logic should detect when a JSON string is provided for the name field and parse it into the appropriate structure before applying variant conversion.

## References

- PR: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/26982
- Skipped test at line 336 in `getBookingResponsesSchema.test.ts`
- Reviewer comment by @volnei: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/26982#pullrequestreview-3819100224

calcom/cal.com#28281.body:
### Issue Summary

While setting up the project locally with yarn dx, the PostgreSQL container (prisma-postgres-1) fails to start and keeps restarting. The root cause is that the volume in 
[packages/prisma/docker-compose.yml](https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docker-compose.yml) is mounted at /var/lib/postgresql/data, but Postgres 18 no longer uses that path — it now stores data under /var/lib/postgresql/18/main (see [docker-library/postgres#1259](https://github.com/docker-library/postgres/pull/1259)).

This means the volume mount needs to be at /var/lib/postgresql instead, so the data actually gets persisted.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Clone the repo and run yarn install
2. Run yarn dx
3. @calcom/prisma#db-up fails — the postgres container is marked unhealthy
4. Running docker logs prisma-postgres-1 shows the error about incompatible data directory

### Actual Results

The container goes into a restart loop. Logs show:

Error: in 18+, these Docker images are configured to store database data in a
       format which is compatible with "pg_ctlcluster" (specifically, using
       major-version-specific directory names).
       Counter to that, there appears to be PostgreSQL data in:
         /var/lib/postgresql/data (unused mount/volume)

       The suggested container configuration for 18+ is to place a single mount
       at /var/lib/postgresql


### Expected Results

yarn dx should start postgres without issues and data should persist across restarts.

### Technical details

- macOS, Docker Desktop
- Postgres 18 (as specified in the docker-compose file)
- Upstream reference: [docker-library/postgres#1259](https://github.com/docker-library/postgres/pull/1259)

The fix is a one-liner in 
packages/prisma/docker-compose.yml
Existing users would also need to clear the old volume with docker compose -f packages/prisma/docker-compose.yml down -v before restarting.

### Evidence
After applying the fix, the container starts fine:

> $ docker ps --filter "name=prisma"
NAMES               STATUS                    PORTS
prisma-postgres-1   Up 35 seconds (healthy)   0.0.0.0:5450->5432/tcp

Happy to open a PR for this.
