calcom/cal.com#28389.body: ## Issue Summary There are several very bad and incorrect Norwegian translations for Cal.com/eu in the context of meeting booking and rescheduling/cancelling. Here are the most important ones I've found so far. See screenshots below for context. ### General **"Event"** Incorrect: "Hendelse" Correct: "Booking" **"Reason"** Incorrect: "Årsak" Correct: "Grunn" (a better word for reason in this context) ### Rescheduling a booking **"Reason for rescheduling"** Incorrect: "Årsak for endring av tidspunkt" Correct: "Grunn for endring av tidspunkt" **"Rescheduled by"** Incorrect: "Omberammet av" Correct: "Tidspunkt endret av" (time/date changed by) Omberammet is not a commonly used word and isn't even correct in this context. **"Your event has been rescheduled"** Incorrect: "Hendelsen din har blitt omplanlagt" Correct: "Bookingen din har blitt endret" ### Cancelling a booking **"Cancel" in the context of cancelling an event** Incorrect: "Avbryt" Correct: "Kanseller" **"Reason for cancelling"** Incorrect: "Grunn for avbestilling" Correct: "Grunn for kansellering" Incorrect: "Avbestillingsårsaken" Correct: "Grunn for kansellering" **"Reason for cancelling will be shared with other guests"** Incorrect: "Avbestillingsårsaken vil bli delt med gjestene" Correct: "Grunn for kansellering vil bli delt med andre deltakere" ("deltakere" ie. participants is better than "gjester" ie. guests in this context) **"Why are you cancelling?"** Incorrect: "Hvorfor avbestiller du?" (avbestill means to cancel goods or services you have bought) Correct: "Hvorfor kansellerer du?" **"Cancel event"** Incorrect: "Avbryt hendelsen" Correct: "Kanseller booking" **"Cancel"** cancel in the context of UI / going back Incorrect: "Glem det" (forget it) Correct: "Avbryt" (cancel in the context of UI / going back) **"This event is cancelled"** Incorrect: "Dette arrangementet er avlyst" Correct: "Denne bookingen er kansellert" ## Evidence

calcom/cal.com#28393.body:
### Issue Summary

After signing up with an incorrect or inaccessible email address, i am  
redirected to the "Check your email" screen with no way 
to go back and correct the mistake. There is no "Use a different email", 
"Go back", or "Try again" button anywhere on the screen and even closing 
the tab and reopening it doesnt clear the issue.

What makes this worse is that the session cookie persists across browser 
restarts. So even if the user closes the browser completely, reopens cal.com, 
and clicks "Sign up with email" again, they are silently redirected back to 
the same "Check your email" screen — still locked to the wrong email — with 
no escape. The only way out is to manually clear browser cookies, which a 
regular user would never know to do.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Go to https://cal.com
2. click sign up with email
3. Enter an email address you do not have access to 
4. Submit the form
5. You are redirected to /auth/verify-email showing "Check your email"
6. there is no button to go back or try a different email — only "Resend email"
7. Close the browser tab completely
8. Reopen cal.com and click "Sign up with email" again
9. You are redirected back to the same "Check your email" screen with no way out



Any other relevant information. For example, why do you consider this a bug and what did you expect to happen instead?

### Actual Results
- After entering a wrong email, there isno way to correct the email
- Closing and reopening the browser does not reset the state 
  always redirected back to the same stuck screen every time i try to 
  sign up again
- i am permanently blocked from completing signup without manually 
  clearing their browser cookies


### Expected Results

-there is a button in the page that says use another email or go back that clears the current 
  unverified session and returns to the signup form
### Technical details

- Browser version: [Brave 1.88.127]
- os: windows 11
- , screen recording, console logs, network requests: You can make a recording with [Bird Eats Bug](https://birdeatsbug.com/).
- Node.js version
- Anything else that you think could be an issue.

### Evidence

i couldnt upload neither a video or a screenshot

This is a bug in the ListSchedules atom. My code is correct. Please escalate to your engineering team.

Confirmed working:

✅ Cal.com self-hosted instance is running
✅ Schedules are created and visible in ListSchedules
✅ CalOAuthProvider configured correctly
✅ CSS imported, dir="ltr" set
✅ getRedirectUrl returns a string
The bug: The ListSchedules atom displays schedules correctly but the getRedirectUrl callback is never invoked when a schedule is clicked. The URL shows # instead of the schedule ID. This is not a configuration issue — the atom is simply not calling the callback internally.

Proof:


javascript
<ListSchedules
  getRedirectUrl={(scheduleId) => {
    console.log("THIS NEVER LOGS:", scheduleId); // ← never called
    return `/availability/${scheduleId}`;
  }}
/>
Console never shows this log. The callback is never fired.

Request:

Please check the source code of ListSchedules atom to verify getRedirectUrl is being called internally
Please provide the correct @calcom/atoms version where this works
Please provide a workaround until this is fixed

calcom/cal.com#28616.body:
## Bug

Webhook subscriber URLs are validated with `z.string().url()` which accepts any valid URL structure regardless of scheme. This means `javascript:`, `ftp:`, `file:`, and other non-HTTPS schemes pass validation and can be stored as webhook endpoints.

## Reproduction

```typescript
import { z } from "zod";
const urlSchema = z.string().url();

// All pass validation - should be rejected for webhooks
urlSchema.parse("javascript:alert(1)");  // passes
urlSchema.parse("ftp://example.com");    // passes
urlSchema.parse("file:///etc/passwd");   // passes
```

## Expected behavior

Webhook URLs should be restricted to `https://` (and optionally `http://` for development):

```typescript
const webhookUrlSchema = z.string().url().refine(
  (url) => url.startsWith("https://") || url.startsWith("http://"),
  { message: "Webhook URL must use HTTPS" }
);
```

## Impact

- A `javascript:` URL stored as a webhook subscriber could be an XSS vector if rendered in the UI
- Non-HTTP schemes are not meaningful for webhook delivery
- `file://` URLs could potentially be used for SSRF if the webhook delivery system follows them

## Suggested fix

Add `.startsWith("https://")` refinement (or an allowlist of `["https://", "http://"]`) to the webhook `subscriberUrl` Zod schema.

Found via automated Vitest chaos monkey testing.
calcom/cal.com#28616.title: fix: webhook URL validation accepts javascript: and non-HTTPS schemes
